name: AXS Retest Hold Derisk v1
edge_mechanism: breakout continuation entered on delayed retest hold with time-based de-risking
thesis: AXS 4h is the structurally different lane for testing whether breakout continuation works better through delayed retest entry and quick de-risking if expansion fails to arrive. The edge should come from avoiding first-bar chase and only participating when reclaimed structure holds.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest holds remain too sparse, if post-retest continuation never accelerates, or if the high-beta lane produces only whipsaw.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A prior expansion pauses, retests the breakout zone, holds above fast value, then resumes in the original direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In weak-trend but upward-biased conditions, delayed retest entries can participate in continuation without paying the worst breakout prices. Time-based de-risking protects against dead second legs.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 4h is suitable as a structurally different lane because breakout/retest behavior is more expressive there, but still slow enough to manage with bar-based rules.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If breakouts in this lane are mostly one-bar events with no second leg, the retest hold will still fail after entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are valid but acceleration is late, keep the retest grammar and adjust de-risk timing before touching entry thresholds.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Not many huge runners, but enough first-target hits with occasional continuation when expansion resumes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 4h and at least one cleaner lane show retest-hold continuation with acceptable density and manageable whipsaw.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse setups, or repeated retest holds that fail to accelerate.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakout, retest, and hold are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest-hold definitions rather than exact breakout candle requirements

validation_basket.why_included: Best stress-test lane for delayed retest continuation in the active basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest-hold setups produce enough density with acceptable whipsaw.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Retest holds are too sparse or fail to accelerate repeatedly.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Patchy but tradable retest continuations with time-based de-risking improving expectancy.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same retest grammar survives on a cleaner, more liquid lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows similar retest continuation, even with smaller range.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup only exists on AXS and disappears on ETH.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer false starts and cleaner retest holds than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the retest-hold logic can transfer to a stronger-trending high-beta lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO keeps the retest grammar and yields stronger second legs.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer entries but stronger continuation legs than AXS.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
